Muntingia calabura, also known as Jamaican cherry, is a small shrub or tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Jamaican cherry include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Jamaican cherry include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Jamaican cherry include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes and digestive issues.
